To create a default text style for the article, define a component text style in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleDocument/componentTextStyles-data.dictionary] object and use the key `default`.

To create a default component text style for a `role`, `d`efine a component text style in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleDocument/componentTextStyles-data.dictionary] and use the key `default-<role>`. For example, if you define a component text style with the key `default-title`, all components with a `role` of `title` use that style, unless you override it.

rawMarkdown:
---
source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ComponentTextStyle
crawled: 2026-03-10T14:19:08Z
---

# ComponentTextStyle

**Object**

The object for defining the style for a text component, including spacing, alignment, and drop caps.

## Declaration

```swift
object ComponentTextStyle
```

## Discussion

Use a `ComponentTextStyle` object to define the text style for an entire text component (such as `body` or `heading`). A `ComponentTextStyle` object can have the same properties as a `TextStyle` object, as well as some additional properties such as a drop cap and text alignment.

To use a `ComponentTextStyle` once, include a `ComponentTextStyle` object as the value of the individual component’s `style` property.

To define a style that multiple components can use:

1. Include a property, with a name that you define and a `ComponentTextStyle` object value, in the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleDocument/componentTextStyles-data.dictionary] object.
2. Use the name you created as the value of the individual component’s `textStyle` property.

To create a default text style for the article, define a component text style in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleDocument/componentTextStyles-data.dictionary] object and use the key `default`.

To create a default component text style for a `role`, `d`efine a component text style in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleDocument/componentTextStyles-data.dictionary] and use the key `default-<role>`. For example, if you define a component text style with the key `default-title`, all components with a `role` of `title` use that style, unless you override it.

For more information about properties, objects, keys, and values, see [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/json-concepts-and-article-structure]. For more about components and roles, see [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/components].

You can use this object in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Text] and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleDocument/componentTextStyles-data.dictionary].

### Example

```json
{
  "componentTextStyles": {
    "exampleStyle": {
      "fontName": "HelveticaNeue",
      "fontSize": 20,
      "dropCapStyle": {
        "numberOfLines": 3,
        "numberOfRaisedLines": 2,
        "numberOfCharacters": 1,
        "fontName": "HelveticaNeue",
        "textColor": "#FFF",
        "backgroundColor": "#000",
        "padding": 5
      }
    }
  },
  "components": [
    {
      "role": "body",
      "text": "This is body text",
      "textStyle": "exampleStyle"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Related Documentation

- **Creating Your First Article**: Create an article with text components and component text styles.

## Text Styles

- **Defining and Applying Text Styles**: Define and apply custom, default, and inline text styles, or use HTML tags or Markdown syntax to style your text.
- **Applying Apple News Format Fonts**: Choose a font family for Apple News Format that’s supported in iOS, iPadOS, and macOS.
- **TextStyle**: The object for defining the text style, such as font family, size, and color, that you can apply to ranges of text.
- **DropCapStyle**: The object for defining the drop cap text style to use in the first paragraph in a text component.
- **ListItemStyle**: The object for defining the style for bulleted or numbered lists in an article.
- **InlineTextStyle**: The object for applying text styling when not using HTML or Markdown formatting.

## Inherits From

- TextStyle
